Days That Slay: Walt Disney: "All our dreams can come true, if we have courage."
Mark Twain: "The best way to cheer yourself up is to cheer somebody else up."
Eleanor Roosevelt: "The future belongs to those who believe in their dreams."
Theodore Roosevelt: "Believe you can and you're halfway there."
Abraham Lincoln: "The best way to predict the future is to create it."
The What Zone: Albert Einstein: "If you can't explain it simply, you don't understand it well enough."
Oscar Wilde: "I can resist everything except temptation."
Benjamin Franklin: "Beer is proof that God loves us and wants us to be happy."
Dorothy Parker: "I like to have a martini, two at the very most."
World Shakers & Icon Makers: Gandhi: "Be the change you wish to see in the world."
Edison: "Genius is one percent inspiration, ninety-nine percent perspiration."
Mandela: "It always seems impossible until it's done."
Curie: "Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood."
Martin Luther King Jr: "Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that."
Beyond Earth: Neil Armstrong: "That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind."
Carl Sagan: "The cosmos is within us. We are made of star-stuff."
Albert Einstein: "The important thing is not to stop questioning."
Stephen Hawking: "Look up at the stars and not down at your feet."
Creature Feature: Jane Goodall: "What you do makes a difference, and you have to decide what kind of difference you want to make."
Charles Darwin: "It is not the strongest that survives, but the most adaptable."
Gerald Durrell: "There’s no such thing as a boring animal. Just a bored observer."
David Attenborough: "We can’t just care about animals we find cute. Every species has a role."
Full Beast Mode: Michael Jordan: "I can accept failure, everyone fails at something. But I can't accept not trying."
Vince Lombardi: "Winners never quit and quitters never win."
Serena Williams: "I really think a champion is defined not by their wins but by how they can recover when they fall."
Muhammad Ali: "Don’t count the days, make the days count."
History's Mic Drop Moments: Julius Caesar: "I came, I saw, I conquered."
Winston Churchill: "Never, never, never give up."
Martin Luther King Jr.: "I have a dream."
Napoleon Bonaparte: "Impossible is a word found only in the dictionary of fools."
John F. Kennedy: "Ask not what your country can do for you — ask what you can do for your country."
Vibes, Beats & Brushes: Pablo Picasso: "Every child is an artist."
Dr. Seuss: "Think left and think right and think low and think high."
Bob Ross: "We don't make mistakes, just happy little accidents."
Taylor Swift: "No matter what happens in life, be good to people. Being good is a wonderful legacy to leave behind."
Mother Nature's Meltdowns: Charles Darwin: "It is not the strongest that survives, but the most adaptable."
Temple Grandin: "Nature is cruel, but we don’t have to be."
Greta Thunberg: "I have learned that you are never too small to make a difference."
Barack Obama: "We are the first generation to feel the effect of climate change and the last generation who can do something about it."
Big Brain Energy: Einstein: "Imagination is more important than knowledge."
Neil deGrasse Tyson: "The good thing about science is that it’s true whether or not you believe in it."
Steve Wozniak: "Never trust a computer you can’t throw out a window."
Isaac Newton: "What we know is a drop, what we don’t know is an ocean."
